title: Completed Access to Information requests - Canada.ca
content:
## Completed Access to Information requests
Government of Canada institutions subject to the Access to Information Act (ATIA) are required to post summaries of processed ATI requests on the Open Canada website.
You can search for Citizenship and Immigration Canada’s summaries, which are available within 30 calendar days after the end of the month, on the “ Completed Access to Information Requests ” webpage.
The list does not include all requests. We did not include any that:
- focused on personal information, or
- focused on third-party proprietary information.
If you find a summary of interest, you can request a copy of the records previously released at no cost.
We will give you the records under the ATIA. They:
- will be in the same format and language in which we released them, and
- will not include any items that were exempted or excluded when we released the records.
For additional information, please see the “ About Access to Information Requests ” webpage.
To request previously released records, select “Make an informal request for” within the applicable summary and complete the request form presented.

## Submit by email or mail
To request previously released records, visit the “ Completed Access to Information Request ” webpage and submit your request by email or by regular mail at:
Access to Information and Privacy Division Informal Request Citizenship and Immigration Canada Ottawa, Ontario K1A 1L1
- Make an access to information request
- Make a privacy request
